codeReview.summary: The implementation introduces the shared stored-row decode seam in production code and reuses it across save-return, get_record, get/list/list_limit foreign reads, while preserving the real RecordId serde contract and avoiding table-qualified-id stripping at the stored-decode boundary.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker transcript references GitNexus impact/detect workflows from repo instructions, but this review environment exposes no GitNexus command/tooling. Workers are falling back to git diff/status review repeatedly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Project instructions in C:\Users\admin\appdb\CLAUDE.md require gitnexus impact/detect_changes, while the reviewed transcript and other mission transcripts record git-diff fallback because the tooling is unavailable.

summary: Reviewed mission artifacts, transcript skeleton, code history, and the production/test diff for the completed feature. The shared decode seam landed in runtime code (core/src/repository/mod.rs), aligns save-return and stored-row decode behavior for nested foreign links, and is backed by the expected RecordId serde-shape proof and focused hydrate regressions.
